talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: NCI Center for Biomedical Informatics and IT
talk_metadata.talk_title: AI Augmentation for Clinical Trial Operations
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "dropping AI into a broken or unsupported environment doesn't fix the problem. It just automates the chaos."
Speaker said: "We have to build the connective tissue around that, such as governance standards, workflows, and human checkpoint."

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: NCI Center for Biomedical Informatics and IT
talk_metadata.talk_title: AI‑Generated Consent Documents and Multilingual Translation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "This is AI‑generated, and this is ready for human review and refinement. It is not intended to go directly to the IRB for approval."
Speaker said: "We can cut translation time in half, about two and a half days, with an AI‑translated document that the human goes in and compares to the English version."

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for AI tools that can map protocol eligibility criteria to common data elements to enable interoperable patient matching.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described the goal of structuring eligibility criteria and linking them to CDEs for trial matching.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide a computational spine that auto‑maps free‑text criteria to standardized ontologies, enabling seamless integration with trial matching engines.

unresolved_questions: How will AI translation quality be validated across languages beyond Spanish?
What metrics will be used to certify AI‑generated consent documents for regulatory acceptance?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: National Cancer Institute, Center for Immuno‑Oncology
talk_metadata.talk_title: Why AI Matters for Patients and Clinical Teams
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The combination of high cognitive load, administrative overhead, and prolonged timeline has real consequences. This limits the number of trials that can open, it slows accrual, and it also constrains the pace of clinical research progress overall."
Speaker said: "We need to avoid unnecessary complexity so that AI tools can safely, reliably move the needle."

crispro_opportunity.description: Opportunity to integrate CrisPRO decision‑support with AI tools that reduce clinician cognitive load in trial enrollment and documentation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized offloading unglamorous tasks to AI so clinicians can focus on higher‑value activities.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can supply predictive models and biomarker insights that complement workflow automation, enhancing clinician decision‑making without adding burden.

unresolved_questions: What specific metrics will demonstrate that AI tools have reduced clinician workload and accelerated trial opening timelines?

assets_to_track: AI workflow automation platforms for trial operations
